texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: I N T R O D U C T I O N
texts.text: I N T R O D U C T I O N

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Welcome to Synplant 2, the next evolution of the software synthesizer that reimagined sound design with a genetic twist. Rather than the traditional knob-twisting and dial-adjusting, Synplant lets you plant sound seeds that evolve into your very own synth patches. The focus is on exploration and discovery, letting your ears guide you through a forest of organic textures and evolving timbres, all waiting to be discovered.
texts.text: Welcome to Synplant 2, the next evolution of the software synthesizer that reimagined sound design with a genetic twist. Rather than the traditional knob-twisting and dial-adjusting, Synplant lets you plant sound seeds that evolve into your very own synth patches. The focus is on exploration and discovery, letting your ears guide you through a forest of organic textures and evolving timbres, all waiting to be discovered.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this second iteration, we've introduced key enhancements to elevate your sound design experience. A standout feature is Genopatch, which employs machine-learning algorithms to analyze your audio samples and generate new Synplant seeds. The results can range from closely mimicking your original sample to unexpected sonic surprises.
texts.text: In this second iteration, we've introduced key enhancements to elevate your sound design experience. A standout feature is Genopatch, which employs machine-learning algorithms to analyze your audio samples and generate new Synplant seeds. The results can range from closely mimicking your original sample to unexpected sonic surprises.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We've also revamped the DNA editor for a more intuitive experience. Unlike its predecessor, which lined up all parameters as identical sliders on a single DNA spiral, the new editor offers a structured layout with graphical representations for various settings like envelopes, oscillator types, and filters. This makes it easier to grasp and manipulate the genetic code of your patches.
texts.text: We've also revamped the DNA editor for a more intuitive experience. Unlike its predecessor, which lined up all parameters as identical sliders on a single DNA spiral, the new editor offers a structured layout with graphical representations for various settings like envelopes, oscillator types, and filters. This makes it easier to grasp and manipulate the genetic code of your patches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Furthermore, the new version introduces additional genes and versatile triggering options, including a layered sound mode. It also features tempo synchronization, monophonic patches, glide, and more. All genes can be automated directly in your DAW, and the audio engine has been updated for better sound quality.
texts.text: Furthermore, the new version introduces additional genes and versatile triggering options, including a layered sound mode. It also features tempo synchronization, monophonic patches, glide, and more. All genes can be automated directly in your DAW, and the audio engine has been updated for better sound quality.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With these advancements, Synplant 2 makes developing your own unique sound easier than ever. Whether you're stumbling upon new sounds through serendipity, drawing inspiration from external samples, or meticulously sculpting parameters with the updated DNA editor, Synplant 2 will have your sonic creations blossom in no time.
texts.text: With these advancements, Synplant 2 makes developing your own unique sound easier than ever. Whether you're stumbling upon new sounds through serendipity, drawing inspiration from external samples, or meticulously sculpting parameters with the updated DNA editor, Synplant 2 will have your sonic creations blossom in no time.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  seed  is  the  core  of  your  sound  in  Synplant.  Drag  within  the  bulb  to  grow branches from this seed. Each branch has a unique timbre. Closest to the seed, all the branches sound identical to the seed, but the farther out you pull a branch, the more different it will sound.
texts.text: The  seed  is  the  core  of  your  sound  in  Synplant.  Drag  within  the  bulb  to  grow branches from this seed. Each branch has a unique timbre. Closest to the seed, all the branches sound identical to the seed, but the farther out you pull a branch, the more different it will sound.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If  you  find  a  branch  you  like,  you  can  cut  it  off  and  re-plant  it  as  a  new  seed  by clicking the Seed Button in the center of the bulb. If none of the branches appeal to you, you can retract one back to its origin to match the original seed sound. Then, click the Seed Button to plant it as a new seed and generate 12 fresh branches.
texts.text: If  you  find  a  branch  you  like,  you  can  cut  it  off  and  re-plant  it  as  a  new  seed  by clicking the Seed Button in the center of the bulb. If none of the branches appeal to you, you can retract one back to its origin to match the original seed sound. Then, click the Seed Button to plant it as a new seed and generate 12 fresh branches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TIP An easy way to fully retract a branch to its root is to click the branch with the COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) key held down.
texts.text: TIP An easy way to fully retract a branch to its root is to click the branch with the COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) key held down.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If  it  is  hard  to  pinpoint  the  exact  desired  length,  hold  down  the SHIFT key  while dragging to obtain a finer resolution.
texts.text: If  it  is  hard  to  pinpoint  the  exact  desired  length,  hold  down  the SHIFT key  while dragging to obtain a finer resolution.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Synplant  automatically  triggers  a  note  to  play  it  whenever  you  click  and  drag  a branch. (If this is undesirable, turn it off using the MIDI Settings window.)
texts.text: Synplant  automatically  triggers  a  note  to  play  it  whenever  you  click  and  drag  a branch. (If this is undesirable, turn it off using the MIDI Settings window.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hold down COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and  click  the Seed Button to generate a new random seed sound. The Atonality slider (see below) affects  the type of seed being created. If the slider is turned down to 0, the seed will typically produce a melodic and playable sound. If it is turned up to 100, the seed is more likely to become an unpitched sound effect.
texts.text: Hold down COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and  click  the Seed Button to generate a new random seed sound. The Atonality slider (see below) affects  the type of seed being created. If the slider is turned down to 0, the seed will typically produce a melodic and playable sound. If it is turned up to 100, the seed is more likely to become an unpitched sound effect.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hold down ALT and click the Seed Button to clone the currently selected branch to all 12 branches. This action makes all branches grow identically so that the sound behaves consistently across all keys. You can also ALT -drag a branch around the bulb to clone it to specific positions.
texts.text: Hold down ALT and click the Seed Button to clone the currently selected branch to all 12 branches. This action makes all branches grow identically so that the sound behaves consistently across all keys. You can also ALT -drag a branch around the bulb to clone it to specific positions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DID YOU KNOW? You can adjust the volumes of individual branches through the Edit Branch Volumes option, accessible by right-clicking on the branches or from the Main Menu . Additionally, you can also auto-normalize all branch levels for equal loudness.
texts.text: DID YOU KNOW? You can adjust the volumes of individual branches through the Edit Branch Volumes option, accessible by right-clicking on the branches or from the Main Menu . Additionally, you can also auto-normalize all branch levels for equal loudness.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Normally, clicking the Key Ring around the bulb plays notes without changing the branch  lengths.  The  exception  is  when  the Bulb  Mode is Layered (see  below) , where clicking the Key Ring buttons toggles individual layers on and off.
texts.text: Normally, clicking the Key Ring around the bulb plays notes without changing the branch  lengths.  The  exception  is  when  the Bulb  Mode is Layered (see  below) , where clicking the Key Ring buttons toggles individual layers on and off.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Press COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and click a Key Ring button to grow a  new  random  branch.  Use  this  to  swap  out  branches  you  don't  like.  The  new branch will sound different when pulled out.
texts.text: Press COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and click a Key Ring button to grow a  new  random  branch.  Use  this  to  swap  out  branches  you  don't  like.  The  new branch will sound different when pulled out.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By dragging the Mod Wheel Ring in and out, you control the Mod Wheel parameter (typically linked to MIDI Controller 1) . The effect varies based on the Wheel Target (described later) . The default target, Growth + , causes all branches to grow simultaneously.
texts.text: By dragging the Mod Wheel Ring in and out, you control the Mod Wheel parameter (typically linked to MIDI Controller 1) . The effect varies based on the Wheel Target (described later) . The default target, Growth + , causes all branches to grow simultaneously.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Rotation Control is the small circle in the periphery of the bulb. By spinning it around, you can change which branches are triggered by which keys on your MIDI keyboard.
texts.text: The Rotation Control is the small circle in the periphery of the bulb. By spinning it around, you can change which branches are triggered by which keys on your MIDI keyboard.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Bulb Mode determines how branches respond to MIDI notes.
texts.text: The Bulb Mode determines how branches respond to MIDI notes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Tuning slider  adjusts the pitch within an octave range. To automatically tune the  sound,  right-click  the  slider  or  select Correct  Tuning from  the Main Menu . While  generally  accurate,  it  may  fail  with  complex  sounds.  For  a  broader  pitch range, modify the a_freq gene in the DNA Editor .  Lower Atonality settings help maintain tuning, particularly with grown-out branches.
texts.text: The Tuning slider  adjusts the pitch within an octave range. To automatically tune the  sound,  right-click  the  slider  or  select Correct  Tuning from  the Main Menu . While  generally  accurate,  it  may  fail  with  complex  sounds.  For  a  broader  pitch range, modify the a_freq gene in the DNA Editor .  Lower Atonality settings help maintain tuning, particularly with grown-out branches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Effect slider controls both the reverb and panning amount. Lower settings produce  a  dry,  monophonic  sound,  while  higher  settings  produce  a  wet,  spacious sound. Stereo panning correlates with branch positions in the bulb: branches on the left side pan to the left and those on the right pan to the right.
texts.text: The Effect slider controls both the reverb and panning amount. Lower settings produce  a  dry,  monophonic  sound,  while  higher  settings  produce  a  wet,  spacious sound. Stereo panning correlates with branch positions in the bulb: branches on the left side pan to the left and those on the right pan to the right.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TIP For more control, adjust the fx_mix and adj_pan genes in the DNA Editor .
texts.text: TIP For more control, adjust the fx_mix and adj_pan genes in the DNA Editor .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Volume slider has a built-in soft clipper that adds distortion at high levels. You can modify the adj_clip gene in the DNA Editor to tame this distortion. To normalize  loudness,  right-click  the  slider  or  choose Normalize  Volume from  the Main Menu .
texts.text: The Volume slider has a built-in soft clipper that adds distortion at high levels. You can modify the adj_clip gene in the DNA Editor to tame this distortion. To normalize  loudness,  right-click  the  slider  or  choose Normalize  Volume from  the Main Menu .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Release slider determines the fade-out time after a key is released. The impact of this slider varies depending on the seed's inherent qualities. The lower Release settings will also fade out the built-in reverb upon key release.
texts.text: The Release slider determines the fade-out time after a key is released. The impact of this slider varies depending on the seed's inherent qualities. The lower Release settings will also fade out the built-in reverb upon key release.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Atonality slider influences the type of sounds produced when growing branches. Lower settings yield more musical and 'playable' sounds, while higher settings introduce atonality and pitch modulation. The effect is subtle on short branches but becomes more pronounced on longer ones. At settings above 50%, notes also become increasingly and randomly detuned, resembling a poorly tuned instrument.
texts.text: The Atonality slider influences the type of sounds produced when growing branches. Lower settings yield more musical and 'playable' sounds, while higher settings introduce atonality and pitch modulation. The effect is subtle on short branches but becomes more pronounced on longer ones. At settings above 50%, notes also become increasingly and randomly detuned, resembling a poorly tuned instrument.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Multiple voices can be played simultaneously. The maximum number of voices  can  be  changed  in  the  MIDI  Settings,  discussed  in  a  separate
texts.text: Multiple voices can be played simultaneously. The maximum number of voices  can  be  changed  in  the  MIDI  Settings,  discussed  in  a  separate

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Mono Only one voice can be played at a time. When a new note is played, the previous one is stopped.
texts.text: Mono Only one voice can be played at a time. When a new note is played, the previous one is stopped.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Legato Similar to Mono , only one voice can be active at a time. However, if you play  a  new  note  while  holding  down  the  previous  one,  the  sound  will transition smoothly without retriggering the voice.
texts.text: Legato Similar to Mono , only one voice can be active at a time. However, if you play  a  new  note  while  holding  down  the  previous  one,  the  sound  will transition smoothly without retriggering the voice.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Mod Wheel can be assigned to various target parameters, and its effect can be scaled using the Mod Wheel Scaling slider (described in the Bottom Sliders section below) .
texts.text: The Mod Wheel can be assigned to various target parameters, and its effect can be scaled using the Mod Wheel Scaling slider (described in the Bottom Sliders section below) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Growth + (the classic Mod Wheel action from Synplant
texts.text: Growth + (the classic Mod Wheel action from Synplant

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This feature attempts to synchronize any rhythmic components of the sound with the host tempo. Available choices for synchronization are: 1/2 , 1/4 D (dotted) , 1/2 T (triplets) , 1/4 , 1/8 D (dotted) , 1/4 T (triplets) , and 1/8 .
texts.text: This feature attempts to synchronize any rhythmic components of the sound with the host tempo. Available choices for synchronization are: 1/2 , 1/4 D (dotted) , 1/2 T (triplets) , 1/4 , 1/8 D (dotted) , 1/4 T (triplets) , and 1/8 .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This slider controls the time for the pitch to glide from one note to another, ranging from 0 to 10 seconds (where 0 seconds means no glide) . In Poly and Mono Voice Modes, the glide occurs between the last note pressed and the next one. In Legato mode, the glide only happens when you press a new key while holding down another.
texts.text: This slider controls the time for the pitch to glide from one note to another, ranging from 0 to 10 seconds (where 0 seconds means no glide) . In Poly and Mono Voice Modes, the glide occurs between the last note pressed and the next one. In Legato mode, the glide only happens when you press a new key while holding down another.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This slider scales the modulation depth of the Mod Wheel , allowing you to control how much the Mod Wheel affects the selected Wheel Target .
texts.text: This slider scales the modulation depth of the Mod Wheel , allowing you to control how much the Mod Wheel affects the selected Wheel Target .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This slider controls how much velocity affects the sound, ranging from 0% to 100%. At 0%, it has no effect, resulting in uniform volume and timbre for all notes. As you increase the slider, velocity adjusts the volume and potentially the sound's character depending on the mod_vel gene.
texts.text: This slider controls how much velocity affects the sound, ranging from 0% to 100%. At 0%, it has no effect, resulting in uniform volume and timbre for all notes. As you increase the slider, velocity adjusts the volume and potentially the sound's character depending on the mod_vel gene.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE This setting does not alter branch selection in Velocity Bulb Mode . A velocity response curve can also be set in the MIDI Settings window for overall control, independent of individual patch settings.
texts.text: NOTE This setting does not alter branch selection in Velocity Bulb Mode . A velocity response curve can also be set in the MIDI Settings window for overall control, independent of individual patch settings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is the main menu button. See the separate section below for descriptions of all available menu items.
texts.text: This is the main menu button. See the separate section below for descriptions of all available menu items.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Saves the current patch as a '.synplant' file on your computer.
texts.text: Saves the current patch as a '.synplant' file on your computer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens the DNA Editor , described in the DNA Editor section below.
texts.text: Opens the DNA Editor , described in the DNA Editor section below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens Genopatch , our revolutionary sample  to patch AI, described in the Genopatch section below.
texts.text: Opens Genopatch , our revolutionary sample  to patch AI, described in the Genopatch section below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reverts or reapplies your recent changes. The undo history is cleared when the editor window is closed.
texts.text: Reverts or reapplies your recent changes. The undo history is cleared when the editor window is closed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These  buttons  allow  you  to  navigate  through  patches  in  the  current  patch  folder quickly.
texts.text: These  buttons  allow  you  to  navigate  through  patches  in  the  current  patch  folder quickly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clicking the patch name unveils a menu listing all available patches in the current folder. This menu includes options to 'Browse Patches…', which opens the Patch File Browser, and Show Current Folder , which reveals the active folder in Windows Explorer or MacOS Finder.
texts.text: Clicking the patch name unveils a menu listing all available patches in the current folder. This menu includes options to 'Browse Patches…', which opens the Patch File Browser, and Show Current Folder , which reveals the active folder in Windows Explorer or MacOS Finder.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If you hold down COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and click the display, Synplant will select a random patch from the currently active folder and load it.
texts.text: If you hold down COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and click the display, Synplant will select a random patch from the currently active folder and load it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Same  as  the Toolbar buttons,  these  options  let  you  revert  or  reapply  recent changes. The undo history is cleared when the editor window is closed.
texts.text: Same  as  the Toolbar buttons,  these  options  let  you  revert  or  reapply  recent changes. The undo history is cleared when the editor window is closed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens the Patch File Browser, described below.
texts.text: Opens the Patch File Browser, described below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Like  the Toolbar button,  this  saves  the  current  patch  as  a '.synplant' file  on your computer.
texts.text: Like  the Toolbar button,  this  saves  the  current  patch  as  a '.synplant' file  on your computer.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: These options let you copy your current patch to the clipboard and paste a patch from the clipboard into Synplant.
texts.text: These options let you copy your current patch to the clipboard and paste a patch from the clipboard into Synplant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens Genopatch , described in a separate section below.
texts.text: Opens Genopatch , described in a separate section below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Plants the currently selected branch as a new seed. Same as clicking the Seed Button . See the Seed and Branches section for more information.
texts.text: Plants the currently selected branch as a new seed. Same as clicking the Seed Button . See the Seed and Branches section for more information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Generates a new random seed for your patch. Same as holding COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and  clicking  the Seed  Button .  See  the  Seed  and  Branches section for more information.
texts.text: Generates a new random seed for your patch. Same as holding COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and  clicking  the Seed  Button .  See  the  Seed  and  Branches section for more information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens the DNA Editor , described in the DNA Editor section below.
texts.text: Opens the DNA Editor , described in the DNA Editor section below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clones the sound of the selected branch to all keys. Same as ALT -clicking the Seed Button . See the Seed and Branches section for more information.
texts.text: Clones the sound of the selected branch to all keys. Same as ALT -clicking the Seed Button . See the Seed and Branches section for more information.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Replaces a branch with a new random one. Same as holding COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and clicking the Key Ring .
texts.text: Replaces a branch with a new random one. Same as holding COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) and clicking the Key Ring .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Automatically tunes your patch to ensure it's in key. Also available by right-clicking the Tuning slider.
texts.text: Automatically tunes your patch to ensure it's in key. Also available by right-clicking the Tuning slider.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Adjusts the volume of your patch to a standard level. Also available by right-clicking the Volume slider.
texts.text: Adjusts the volume of your patch to a standard level. Also available by right-clicking the Volume slider.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Levels the volume across all branches of your seed. Also available by right-clicking a branch.
texts.text: Levels the volume across all branches of your seed. Also available by right-clicking a branch.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens a layer that allows you to manually adjust each branch's volume. Also available by right-clicking a branch.
texts.text: Opens a layer that allows you to manually adjust each branch's volume. Also available by right-clicking a branch.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens a layer with MIDI CC boxes, allowing you to map Synplant functions to your MIDI controller. Click a MIDI CC box to activate 'MIDI learn' (if your host application supports it) . Drag or right-click to set a numeric value.
texts.text: Opens a layer with MIDI CC boxes, allowing you to map Synplant functions to your MIDI controller. Click a MIDI CC box to activate 'MIDI learn' (if your host application supports it) . Drag or right-click to set a numeric value.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens a window to configure MIDI settings. See MIDI Settings section below.
texts.text: Opens a window to configure MIDI settings. See MIDI Settings section below.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Synplant  offers  16  slots  for  loading  patches,  allowing  quick  transitions  between sounds. You can switch between these patches using MIDI program change commands if your host supports it, and it is enabled in the MIDI Settings window. The program list is persistent; a new instance of Synplant will automatically load with the previously selected 16 patches.
texts.text: Synplant  offers  16  slots  for  loading  patches,  allowing  quick  transitions  between sounds. You can switch between these patches using MIDI program change commands if your host supports it, and it is enabled in the MIDI Settings window. The program list is persistent; a new instance of Synplant will automatically load with the previously selected 16 patches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens a dialog that allows you to enter your registration key to unlock the full version of Synplant.
texts.text: Opens a dialog that allows you to enter your registration key to unlock the full version of Synplant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Runs the introductory tutorial shown at the first launch to get you acquainted with Synplant.
texts.text: Runs the introductory tutorial shown at the first launch to get you acquainted with Synplant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Toggles the feature that automatically checks for software updates.
texts.text: Toggles the feature that automatically checks for software updates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Allows you to scale the entire user interface. The zoom levels range between 50% and 200%.
texts.text: Allows you to scale the entire user interface. The zoom levels range between 50% and 200%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When you choose to open a patch, a familiar MacOS/Windows file browser will appear, but with added functionalities. Firstly, you can preview any patch instantly by clicking it and playing it using your MIDI keyboard without loading it into the plug-in and closing the browser.
texts.text: When you choose to open a patch, a familiar MacOS/Windows file browser will appear, but with added functionalities. Firstly, you can preview any patch instantly by clicking it and playing it using your MIDI keyboard without loading it into the plug-in and closing the browser.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Secondly, two buttons at the bottom allow quick navigation between the 'Factory Patches' and 'User Patches' folders. The 'User Patches' folder initially defaults to a location under your 'user folder,' but if you save a patch to a different folder, it will update to that location.
texts.text: Secondly, two buttons at the bottom allow quick navigation between the 'Factory Patches' and 'User Patches' folders. The 'User Patches' folder initially defaults to a location under your 'user folder,' but if you save a patch to a different folder, it will update to that location.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Inside the 'Factory Patches' folder, you'll find sub-folders organized as follows:
texts.text: Inside the 'Factory Patches' folder, you'll find sub-folders organized as follows:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patches are sorted into categories like Basses, Pads, etc.
texts.text: Patches are sorted into categories like Basses, Pads, etc.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patches are organized by their creators.
texts.text: Patches are organized by their creators.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patches are grouped based on their respective sound packages.
texts.text: Patches are grouped based on their respective sound packages.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DNA Editor window is where you can fine-tune the genetic makeup of your sound. The DNA spiral contains 48 genes, each represented by a circle on a horizontal line. To adjust a gene, click its circle and drag it left or right. Drag the mouse up or down (or SHIFT -click) for more precise adjustment. Hold COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) to reset the value to its default (0.5 for most genes).
texts.text: The DNA Editor window is where you can fine-tune the genetic makeup of your sound. The DNA spiral contains 48 genes, each represented by a circle on a horizontal line. To adjust a gene, click its circle and drag it left or right. Drag the mouse up or down (or SHIFT -click) for more precise adjustment. Hold COMMAND (Mac) or CONTROL (Windows) to reset the value to its default (0.5 for most genes).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DNA spiral also visualizes how branches grow from the base seed settings as colorful lines overlaying the spiral. Observe that the genes control the seed settings only; branches grow randomly.
texts.text: The DNA spiral also visualizes how branches grow from the base seed settings as colorful lines overlaying the spiral. Observe that the genes control the seed settings only; branches grow randomly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The DNA spiral is segmented into three outlined boxes at the top, middle, and bottom. Clicking inside these boxes switches the view to different sections displayed to the right of the spiral:
texts.text: The DNA spiral is segmented into three outlined boxes at the top, middle, and bottom. Clicking inside these boxes switches the view to different sections displayed to the right of the spiral:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The knobs in the detailed sections on the right-hand side mirror the horizontal controls in the DNA spiral. Information about the gene you're hovering over is displayed at the bottom of the DNA window.
texts.text: The knobs in the detailed sections on the right-hand side mirror the horizontal controls in the DNA spiral. Information about the gene you're hovering over is displayed at the bottom of the DNA window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Right-clicking a gene knob gives you a context menu with options to flip its value, input an exact value as text or reset the gene to its default setting. You can automate all knobs in your DAW and map them to MIDI controls.
texts.text: Right-clicking a gene knob gives you a context menu with options to flip its value, input an exact value as text or reset the gene to its default setting. You can automate all knobs in your DAW and map them to MIDI controls.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Synplant's genes are not traditional synthesizer parameters. They have complex relationships  and  have  been  extensively  tuned  to  generate  useful  sounds  through random mutations. All gene settings range from 0.0 to 1.0, and virtually all genes have a default setting of 0.5.
texts.text: Synplant's genes are not traditional synthesizer parameters. They have complex relationships  and  have  been  extensively  tuned  to  generate  useful  sounds  through random mutations. All gene settings range from 0.0 to 1.0, and virtually all genes have a default setting of 0.5.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Genopatch is a groundbreaking AI that crafts synth patches from audio recordings. It achieves this by making educated guesses about optimal synth settings, listening to the outcomes, and iteratively refining them to match the reference audio better.
texts.text: Genopatch is a groundbreaking AI that crafts synth patches from audio recordings. It achieves this by making educated guesses about optimal synth settings, listening to the outcomes, and iteratively refining them to match the reference audio better.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Using Genopatch is straightforward. You start by selecting a source sample, choosing a segment of up to two seconds from that sample, and then hitting start. You'll then see four strands sprouting upwards, each generating patches. The higher they grow, the closer their solutions (represented by circles) will be to your source audio.
texts.text: Using Genopatch is straightforward. You start by selecting a source sample, choosing a segment of up to two seconds from that sample, and then hitting start. You'll then see four strands sprouting upwards, each generating patches. The higher they grow, the closer their solutions (represented by circles) will be to your source audio.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To preview a solution, click on one of the circles. The selected patch will load into Synplant, and you can play it on your keyboard. To save your favorite patches, click the Save button in the Toolbar , just as usual. (Hold down the SHIFT key if you want
texts.text: To preview a solution, click on one of the circles. The selected patch will load into Synplant, and you can play it on your keyboard. To save your favorite patches, click the Save button in the Toolbar , just as usual. (Hold down the SHIFT key if you want

texts.label: text
texts.orig: to select a solution without triggering it.)
texts.text: to select a solution without triggering it.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Initially, the generated sounds may vary widely and not closely resemble the reference sample. However, as the strands grow, they converge toward more accurate solutions, each with unique characteristics. Once a strand reaches the top, it stops growing, as the solutions tend to become increasingly similar.
texts.text: Initially, the generated sounds may vary widely and not closely resemble the reference sample. However, as the strands grow, they converge toward more accurate solutions, each with unique characteristics. Once a strand reaches the top, it stops growing, as the solutions tend to become increasingly similar.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: While Synplant is highly versatile, it can't replicate every sound perfectly due to its synthetic nature and the limitations of its 48-gene DNA. But that's often the point. Sometimes you get a perfect match with the original sound, and other times you end up with something entirely new and unexpected.
texts.text: While Synplant is highly versatile, it can't replicate every sound perfectly due to its synthetic nature and the limitations of its 48-gene DNA. But that's often the point. Sometimes you get a perfect match with the original sound, and other times you end up with something entirely new and unexpected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The entire operation runs on your computer's CPU, requiring no internet connection. The speed at which Genopatch produces new results depends on your processor's capabilities.  Although it  utilizes  all  available  CPU  power,  it's  designed  to  minimize system lag. Closing the Genopatch window will pause the process, allowing you to resume later. However, closing the project in your DAW will erase all found solutions, requiring you to start anew.
texts.text: The entire operation runs on your computer's CPU, requiring no internet connection. The speed at which Genopatch produces new results depends on your processor's capabilities.  Although it  utilizes  all  available  CPU  power,  it's  designed  to  minimize system lag. Closing the Genopatch window will pause the process, allowing you to resume later. However, closing the project in your DAW will erase all found solutions, requiring you to start anew.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: TIP After you create a patch with Genopatch, you might want to adjust the velocity and keyboard response using the Velocity slider and the env_kf , mod_vel , and flt_kf genes.
texts.text: TIP After you create a patch with Genopatch, you might want to adjust the velocity and keyboard response using the Velocity slider and the env_kf , mod_vel , and flt_kf genes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Opens a sample browser to load your reference sound. The browser features a Play button and an Auto Play switch for sample previews. It supports WAV and AIFF files of any sample rate and bit resolution. Samples longer than 10 minutes will be truncated. You can also drag and drop a sample file directly into the Synplant window to load it into Genopatch. Some DAWs even allow drag-and-drop from an audio track.
texts.text: Opens a sample browser to load your reference sound. The browser features a Play button and an Auto Play switch for sample previews. It supports WAV and AIFF files of any sample rate and bit resolution. Samples longer than 10 minutes will be truncated. You can also drag and drop a sample file directly into the Synplant window to load it into Genopatch. Some DAWs even allow drag-and-drop from an audio track.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Previews the selected segment of the reference sound.
texts.text: Previews the selected segment of the reference sound.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When off, Genopatch will tune the patches to match the pitch of the source sample when you press the C3 key. Turning it on will adjust the pitch based on the detected pitch in the reference sample.
texts.text: When off, Genopatch will tune the patches to match the pitch of the source sample when you press the C3 key. Turning it on will adjust the pitch based on the detected pitch in the reference sample.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click to start the magic. Click again to pause. (Closing the Genopatch window will also pause the process.)
texts.text: Click to start the magic. Click again to pause. (Closing the Genopatch window will also pause the process.)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clears all results, allowing you to select a new segment from the reference sample and start over.
texts.text: Clears all results, allowing you to select a new segment from the reference sample and start over.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When on, each new solution will automatically play upon discovery. It turns off when you manually select a solution.
texts.text: When on, each new solution will automatically play upon discovery. It turns off when you manually select a solution.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click and drag to scroll horizontally and zoom vertically.
texts.text: Click and drag to scroll horizontally and zoom vertically.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Adjust the left and right ends to select a new range, or click and drag to move the entire range.
texts.text: Adjust the left and right ends to select a new range, or click and drag to move the entire range.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Swaps the waveform display for an info display, showing detected pitch, solution testing rate, and additional details about selected solutions.
texts.text: Swaps the waveform display for an info display, showing detected pitch, solution testing rate, and additional details about selected solutions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click to discard an individual strand and restart. Holding down the ALT key  while clicking invokes a special mode, using the currently loaded Synplant patch as the search base. The solutions will gradually shift  from  the  source  patch  towards  the reference audio as the strand grows.
texts.text: Click to discard an individual strand and restart. Holding down the ALT key  while clicking invokes a special mode, using the currently loaded Synplant patch as the search base. The solutions will gradually shift  from  the  source  patch  towards  the reference audio as the strand grows.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The MIDI Settings window in Synplant Version 2 lets you fine-tune how the software interacts with MIDI data. Each Synplant instance has unique settings saved within your project but not in individual patch files. These settings can also be saved and loaded as separate .scmc files.
texts.text: The MIDI Settings window in Synplant Version 2 lets you fine-tune how the software interacts with MIDI data. Each Synplant instance has unique settings saved within your project but not in individual patch files. These settings can also be saved and loaded as separate .scmc files.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Set the MIDI channel that this instance of Synplant will respond to.
texts.text: Set the MIDI channel that this instance of Synplant will respond to.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Choose the MIDI channel Synplant will use to send out MIDI notes when you play branches from the user interface. Setting this to Off will disable note playback when editing branches.
texts.text: Choose the MIDI channel Synplant will use to send out MIDI notes when you play branches from the user interface. Setting this to Off will disable note playback when editing branches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Select the velocity curve from options: Low, Normal, High, or Fixed.
texts.text: Select the velocity curve from options: Low, Normal, High, or Fixed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Choose the pressure setting: Soft, Normal, Hard, or Off. This routes MIDI pressure to the Mod Wheel and is useful for MPE compatibility.
texts.text: Choose the pressure setting: Soft, Normal, Hard, or Off. This routes MIDI pressure to the Mod Wheel and is useful for MPE compatibility.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Set  the  minimum  polyphony  level.  This  setting  is  used  in  all Bulb  Modes except Layered .
texts.text: Set  the  minimum  polyphony  level.  This  setting  is  used  in  all Bulb  Modes except Layered .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Set the maximum polyphony level. In Layered mode, polyphony is multiplied by the number of active layers up to this limit.
texts.text: Set the maximum polyphony level. In Layered mode, polyphony is multiplied by the number of active layers up to this limit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Adjust the range of the pitch wheel in semitones.
texts.text: Adjust the range of the pitch wheel in semitones.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Tune the master pitch between 340 to 540, accommodating those who prefer nonstandard tuning like 432Hz.
texts.text: Tune the master pitch between 340 to 540, accommodating those who prefer nonstandard tuning like 432Hz.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Pitch Wheel Affects Only Held Notes
texts.text: Pitch Wheel Affects Only Held Notes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When checked, only notes that you hold down will be affected by the pitch wheel. This is useful for MPE compatibility.
texts.text: When checked, only notes that you hold down will be affected by the pitch wheel. This is useful for MPE compatibility.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If  unchecked,  playing  notes  won't  automatically  select  branches,  allowing  you  to edit while playing a sequence.
texts.text: If  unchecked,  playing  notes  won't  automatically  select  branches,  allowing  you  to edit while playing a sequence.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Enable or disable Synplant's response to MIDI program change messages. The Edit button takes you to the MIDI Program List window.
texts.text: Enable or disable Synplant's response to MIDI program change messages. The Edit button takes you to the MIDI Program List window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Clicking this button resets all MIDI configuration settings to their default values.
texts.text: Clicking this button resets all MIDI configuration settings to their default values.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Use this button to load a previously saved MIDI configuration.
texts.text: Use this button to load a previously saved MIDI configuration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click this button to save the current MIDI configuration settings.
texts.text: Click this button to save the current MIDI configuration settings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This button sets the current configuration as the default for new instances of Synplant.
texts.text: This button sets the current configuration as the default for new instances of Synplant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: While Synplant does not fully support the MPE specification, it does allow for pernote MIDI controllers and expressions. Recommended settings for MPE compatibility are:
texts.text: While Synplant does not fully support the MPE specification, it does allow for pernote MIDI controllers and expressions. Recommended settings for MPE compatibility are:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Synplant 2 has improvements in the audio engine that may result in subtle sound differences compared to version 1. While these changes are often minor, they can be more pronounced under specific settings.
texts.text: Synplant 2 has improvements in the audio engine that may result in subtle sound differences compared to version 1. While these changes are often minor, they can be more pronounced under specific settings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When you load patches and songs saved with Synplant version 1, you will see a warning that Synplant is running in 'compatibility mode'. In this mode, Synplant reproduces the legacy sound of version 1 precisely without the DSP improvements of version 2.
texts.text: When you load patches and songs saved with Synplant version 1, you will see a warning that Synplant is running in 'compatibility mode'. In this mode, Synplant reproduces the legacy sound of version 1 precisely without the DSP improvements of version 2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can choose to either dismiss the warning or upgrade the patch. If you upgrade the patch and it does not sound as expected, you can revert the changes using the Undo button.
texts.text: You can choose to either dismiss the warning or upgrade the patch. If you upgrade the patch and it does not sound as expected, you can revert the changes using the Undo button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The minimum requirements for installing and running Synplant are:
texts.text: The minimum requirements for installing and running Synplant are:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A host that supports 64-bit VST® 2.4, or VST3 plug-ins
texts.text: A host that supports 64-bit VST® 2.4, or VST3 plug-ins

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A host that supports 64-bit VST 2.4, VST3, or AudioUnit 2 plug-ins
texts.text: A host that supports 64-bit VST 2.4, VST3, or AudioUnit 2 plug-ins

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Thanks to all our fabulous beta testers!
texts.text: Thanks to all our fabulous beta testers!

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed  a  bug  where  loading  a  wav  file  from  a  directory  with  Unicode  characters caused the file browser to display an error and not open on subsequent attempts.
texts.text: Fixed  a  bug  where  loading  a  wav  file  from  a  directory  with  Unicode  characters caused the file browser to display an error and not open on subsequent attempts.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · General  improvements to Unicode character handling in file  paths,  resolving  issues like those in the "Favorite Button" mod.
texts.text: General  improvements to Unicode character handling in file  paths,  resolving  issues like those in the "Favorite Button" mod.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed a compatibility bug with slow "mod_sh" settings in "backwards compatibility mode".
texts.text: Fixed a compatibility bug with slow "mod_sh" settings in "backwards compatibility mode".

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed broken control-click to load random patch on Windows.
texts.text: Fixed broken control-click to load random patch on Windows.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Mod-wheel (and  other  CC  changes) work  in  the  file  browser  preview  also  in  the VST3 version.
texts.text: Mod-wheel (and  other  CC  changes) work  in  the  file  browser  preview  also  in  the VST3 version.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Genopatch claims slightly less CPU in the Windows version.
texts.text: Genopatch claims slightly less CPU in the Windows version.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Using more modern file browsers on Windows.
texts.text: Using more modern file browsers on Windows.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Improved error handling in Windows installer.
texts.text: Improved error handling in Windows installer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · New drag and drop implementation on Windows for FL Studio compatibility.
texts.text: New drag and drop implementation on Windows for FL Studio compatibility.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · If you update a version 1 patch and save it as a ".synplant" file, Synplant will ask if you want to remove the old ".synp" file.
texts.text: If you update a version 1 patch and save it as a ".synplant" file, Synplant will ask if you want to remove the old ".synp" file.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Prepared support for "explicit branch morphs" in the audio engine.
texts.text: Prepared support for "explicit branch morphs" in the audio engine.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Support for "mods" (scripts that installs modifications on the UI) .
texts.text: Support for "mods" (scripts that installs modifications on the UI) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Bug-fixes and improvements in Javascript back-end.
texts.text: Bug-fixes and improvements in Javascript back-end.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Slightly improved the neural net in Genopatch.
texts.text: Slightly improved the neural net in Genopatch.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Introducing Genopatch, our sample to patch AI.
texts.text: Introducing Genopatch, our sample to patch AI.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rewritten audio engine: new genes and improved DSP algorithms.
texts.text: Rewritten audio engine: new genes and improved DSP algorithms.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · New DNA editor with a structured parameter layout and graphs.
texts.text: New DNA editor with a structured parameter layout and graphs.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · All genes can be automated and mapped to MIDI CC
texts.text: All genes can be automated and mapped to MIDI CC

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · New "bulb modes" give you alternative ways to trigger the different branches.
texts.text: New "bulb modes" give you alternative ways to trigger the different branches.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Bug fixes, 'under the hood' maintenance, and improvements.
texts.text: Bug fixes, 'under the hood' maintenance, and improvements.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Made a workaround to handle a rare Windows problem with generating a unique machine-id.
texts.text: Made a workaround to handle a rare Windows problem with generating a unique machine-id.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Got rid of potential audio buffer under-runs (causing clicks and pops) on first playback.
texts.text: Got rid of potential audio buffer under-runs (causing clicks and pops) on first playback.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · New algorithm for the 'system unique identifier' used for authorization. Hopefully fixing the problem where the plug-in became unregistered spontaneously.
texts.text: New algorithm for the 'system unique identifier' used for authorization. Hopefully fixing the problem where the plug-in became unregistered spontaneously.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed a bug that could leave temporary files behind when saving and replacing files.
texts.text: Fixed a bug that could leave temporary files behind when saving and replacing files.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Mac) Solved a compatibility problem with DAWs that are built with recent Apple SDK's, e.g., Cubase 10.5.
texts.text: (Mac) Solved a compatibility problem with DAWs that are built with recent Apple SDK's, e.g., Cubase 10.5.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Mac) 'Go to folder' buttons in browser now work in Catalina.
texts.text: (Mac) 'Go to folder' buttons in browser now work in Catalina.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Mac) 64-bit  Audio  Unit  no  longer  depends  on  the  'Component  Manager'.  This means you should not need to restart after installation.
texts.text: (Mac) 64-bit  Audio  Unit  no  longer  depends  on  the  'Component  Manager'.  This means you should not need to restart after installation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Mac) Preferences  and  registration  data  is  now  shared  with  'sandboxed'  DAWs like GarageBand (meaning Authenticator works with these DAWs too) .
texts.text: (Mac) Preferences  and  registration  data  is  now  shared  with  'sandboxed'  DAWs like GarageBand (meaning Authenticator works with these DAWs too) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Mac) Fixed a problem where under certain conditions the preferences data could stay locked if the DAW crashed, requiring a full system restart.
texts.text: (Mac) Fixed a problem where under certain conditions the preferences data could stay locked if the DAW crashed, requiring a full system restart.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · (Windows) No longer fails showing the file popup if there are more than 1000 files in the directory (file popups are however limited to 500 entries) .
texts.text: (Windows) No longer fails showing the file popup if there are more than 1000 files in the directory (file popups are however limited to 500 entries) .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Lots of other minor bug and compatibility fixes.
texts.text: Lots of other minor bug and compatibility fixes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed: shift-clicking for dragging any of the arced sliders around the plant or any branch on the plant always resulted in dragging the Effect slider.
texts.text: Fixed: shift-clicking for dragging any of the arced sliders around the plant or any branch on the plant always resulted in dragging the Effect slider.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Also fixed: If Windows had been running (without restart) for more than 24 days straight, Synplant didn't properly display 'tutorial bubbles' or 'tooltip hints'.
texts.text: Also fixed: If Windows had been running (without restart) for more than 24 days straight, Synplant didn't properly display 'tutorial bubbles' or 'tooltip hints'.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Open browsers now feature two buttons to quickly take you to factory and user preset directories.
texts.text: Open browsers now feature two buttons to quickly take you to factory and user preset directories.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Supports Sonic Charge Authenticator for easier registration.
texts.text: Supports Sonic Charge Authenticator for easier registration.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Compiled  with  latest  compilers,  frameworks  and  libraries  for  improved  stability and compatibility.
texts.text: Compiled  with  latest  compilers,  frameworks  and  libraries  for  improved  stability and compatibility.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moved documentation and factory patch location on Windows from DLL directory to \Program Files\Sonic Charge
texts.text: Moved documentation and factory patch location on Windows from DLL directory to \Program Files\Sonic Charge

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Name of DLL (Windows) and 'bundle' (Mac) changed from SynplantVST/SynplantAU to Synplant.
texts.text: Name of DLL (Windows) and 'bundle' (Mac) changed from SynplantVST/SynplantAU to Synplant.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Implemented support for the  'all  notes  off  midi  message'.  We  have  discovered that some hosts (most importantly Cakewalk Sonar) requires this to stop notes from playing.  In  other  words,  the  hanging  note  problems  in  Sonar  should  have  been fixed now.
texts.text: Implemented support for the  'all  notes  off  midi  message'.  We  have  discovered that some hosts (most importantly Cakewalk Sonar) requires this to stop notes from playing.  In  other  words,  the  hanging  note  problems  in  Sonar  should  have  been fixed now.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Improved  compatibility  with  older  VST  2.3  hosts,  including  FXpansion's  RTAS wrapper. VST 2.4 is still the official requirement, simply because we need to put a limit on the extent of our guarantees. However, we will continue doing our best to provide 'unofficial  support'  for  as  many  hosts  as  we  possibly  can.  In  short,  we expect this version of Synplant to work well with the RTAS wrapper.
texts.text: Improved  compatibility  with  older  VST  2.3  hosts,  including  FXpansion's  RTAS wrapper. VST 2.4 is still the official requirement, simply because we need to put a limit on the extent of our guarantees. However, we will continue doing our best to provide 'unofficial  support'  for  as  many  hosts  as  we  possibly  can.  In  short,  we expect this version of Synplant to work well with the RTAS wrapper.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fixed a rare but serious problem with the GUI freezing up on Mac (never to defrost again, never to come back to life) . Turned out to be a bug in Synplant in combination with some unexpected behavior from OS X.
texts.text: Fixed a rare but serious problem with the GUI freezing up on Mac (never to defrost again, never to come back to life) . Turned out to be a bug in Synplant in combination with some unexpected behavior from OS X.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Some  minor  updates  to  the  internal  code  libraries  that  will  improve  stability  in general.
texts.text: Some  minor  updates  to  the  internal  code  libraries  that  will  improve  stability  in general.

texts.orig: The contractor/manufacturer for Sonic Charge Synplant is:
texts.text: The contractor/manufacturer for Sonic Charge Synplant is:

table_cells.text: Each branch corresponds to a specific note within an octave: C, C#, D, etc., up to B.
Branches are triggered by different velocity ranges. For example, the bottom branch responds to the highest velocities (117-127) .
Branches are mapped to six-semitone ranges (C to F, F# to B) , from the keyboard's bottom to the top.

Enables multiple branches to be triggered simultaneously for rich, layered sounds. The Atonality setting detunes these layers, even when fully contracted. Note that enabling many layers reduces po- lyphony. (You can adjust the maximum polyphony in the MIDI Set- tings window, described later.)

grid.text: Each branch corresponds to a specific note within an octave: C, C#, D, etc., up to B.
Branches are triggered by different velocity ranges. For example, the bottom branch responds to the highest velocities (117-127) .
Branches are mapped to six-semitone ranges (C to F, F# to B) , from the keyboard's bottom to the top.

Enables multiple branches to be triggered simultaneously for rich, layered sounds. The Atonality setting detunes these layers, even when fully contracted. Note that enabling many layers reduces po- lyphony. (You can adjust the maximum polyphony in the MIDI Set- tings window, described later.)

table_cells.text: Contracts the branches when the Mod Wheel is turned up.
Opens the filter as the Mod Wheel is turned up, starting from the current patch setting. The related gene for this is flt_freq .
Starts with a closed filter and opens it up to the current patch settings as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The related gene is flt_freq .
Slows down the timing of the sound as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The affected genes are env_time and env_loop .
Speeds up the timing of the sound as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The affected genes are env_time and env_loop .
Modulates the frequency modulation amount. The related gene is fm_amt .
Modulates the vibrato/tremolo amount. The related gene is lfo_amt .
Increases the effect amount with the Mod Wheel , similar to turning up the Effect slider.
Increases the volume with the Mod Wheel , similar to turning up the Volume slider.

grid.text: Contracts the branches when the Mod Wheel is turned up.
Opens the filter as the Mod Wheel is turned up, starting from the current patch setting. The related gene for this is flt_freq .
Starts with a closed filter and opens it up to the current patch settings as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The related gene is flt_freq .
Slows down the timing of the sound as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The affected genes are env_time and env_loop .
Speeds up the timing of the sound as the Mod Wheel is turned up. The affected genes are env_time and env_loop .
Modulates the frequency modulation amount. The related gene is fm_amt .
Modulates the vibrato/tremolo amount. The related gene is lfo_amt .
Increases the effect amount with the Mod Wheel , similar to turning up the Effect slider.
Increases the volume with the Mod Wheel , similar to turning up the Volume slider.

table_cells.text: Controls the total duration of the sound. Above 0.9, the envelope will have infinite duration.
Controls the looping time of the envelope. A longer loop time than env_time means no looping will occur.
Determines the tilt of the envelope. Smaller values mean quicker attack and longer decay, while higher values mean slower attack and faster decay.
Controls the key follow of the envelope. Settings above 0.5 make the envelope shorter with high keys and longer with low keys.
Shapes the attack phase of the volume envelope. A value of 0.5 gives a linear attack.
Shapes the decay phase of the volume envelope. A value of 0.5 gives a linear decay.
Sets the sustain level of the sound. A value under 0.5 means no sustain.
Introduces an additional volume fade that begins after the initial attack phase. Settings above 0.75 activate the fade.
Shapes the attack phase of the modulation envelope. A value of 0.5 gives a linear attack.
Shapes the decay phase of the modulation envelope. A value of 0.5 gives a linear decay.
Sets the sample-and-hold frequency of the modulation envelope. Only very high values will acti- vate the effect.
Determines how velocity affects the modulation envelope. Lower settings lower the sensitivity; below 0.1, velocity does not affect modulation.
Adjusts the vibrato/tremolo effect. Below 0.5, Oscillator A and B are modulated in opposite direc- tions.
Decides the balance between vibrato (0.0) and tremolo (1.0) .
Settings above 0.5 introduce a delay before the LFO starts modulating the sound.

Controls the looping time of the envelope. A longer loop time than env_time means no looping will occur.
Determines the tilt of the envelope. Smaller values mean quicker attack and longer decay, while higher values mean slower attack and faster decay.
Controls the key follow of the envelope. Settings above 0.5 make the envelope shorter with high keys and longer with low keys.

Determines how velocity affects the modulation envelope. Lower settings lower the sensitivity; below 0.1, velocity does not affect modulation.
Adjusts the vibrato/tremolo effect. Below 0.5, Oscillator A and B are modulated in opposite direc- tions.
Decides the balance between vibrato (0.0) and tremolo (1.0) .
Settings above 0.5 introduce a delay before the LFO starts modulating the sound.

table_cells.text: Controls the waveform shape and timbre of Oscillator A.
Adjusts the mix of noise in Oscillator A.
Determines the pitch modulation amount from the modulation envelope.
Changes the character of the noise in Oscillator A.
Controls the pitch of Oscillator A (also affects Oscillator B and filter cutoff) .
Affects how the modulation envelope modulates the FM amount.
Controls the frequency modulation amount of Oscillator A by Oscillator B.
Determines how the modulation envelope modulates the mix between Oscillator A and B.
Changes the mix between Oscillator A and B.
Controls the waveform shape and timbre of Oscillator B.
Adjusts the mix of noise in Oscillator B.
Determines the pitch modulation amount from the modulation envelope.
Controls the pitch of Oscillator B relative to the pitch of Oscillator A.
Sets the sample-and-hold rate of Oscillator B. Values above 0.5 set a fixed rate, while values below sync the rate to Oscillator B's frequency.

grid.text: Controls the waveform shape and timbre of Oscillator A.
Adjusts the mix of noise in Oscillator A.
Determines the pitch modulation amount from the modulation envelope.
Changes the character of the noise in Oscillator A.
Controls the pitch of Oscillator A (also affects Oscillator B and filter cutoff) .
Affects how the modulation envelope modulates the FM amount.
Controls the frequency modulation amount of Oscillator A by Oscillator B.
Determines how the modulation envelope modulates the mix between Oscillator A and B.
Changes the mix between Oscillator A and B.
Controls the waveform shape and timbre of Oscillator B.
Adjusts the mix of noise in Oscillator B.
Determines the pitch modulation amount from the modulation envelope.
Controls the pitch of Oscillator B relative to the pitch of Oscillator A.
Sets the sample-and-hold rate of Oscillator B. Values above 0.5 set a fixed rate, while values below sync the rate to Oscillator B's frequency.

table_cells.text: Morphs the filter type from bandpass to dual lowpass (serial and parallel) to notch.
Controls the 'q value' (or 'resonance') of the filters.
Determines the cutoff modulation amount from the modulation envelope.
Sets the separation between the cutoff frequencies of the two internal filter stages.
Controls the cutoff frequency relative to the frequency of Oscillator A.
Determines how filter cutoff changes over the keyboard. Below 0.25, the cutoff is fixed; above 0.75, it follows the keyboard exactly.
Adjusts the level of saturation on the voice.
Sets the dry/wet mix of the built-in effect.
Applies an envelope to the reverb amount when set above 0.66.
Changes the length of the reverberation.
Controls how the reverb dampens higher frequencies when it decays.
Changes the size of the perceived reverberation space.
Controls the output low-shelf filter that adjusts the bass frequency content of the sound.
Controls the output high-shelf filter that adjusts the treble frequency content of the sound.
Adjusts the panning of the branches around the center seed.
Adjusts the gain of the 'soft clipper', which is applied after the master volume.
